_note: hoop/v095 CHATTER — faction × story-phase ambient lines for the procedural crowd. NOT hand-authored per NPC: every tree-less crowd NPC picks a line by (its faction tag, the active phase, a per-NPC seed), so the whole Nave reacts to the story with a few dozen lines. Deterministic, zero inference (the 'no LLM in the hot path' rule). The phase is the highest one whose `when` the player's state satisfies. This file is also the artifact the offline pregen lane can later grow (more lines / more phases) — the hot path (chatter.js) never changes. Principals (Olo/Sevin/Solen) are hand-authored with gated dialogue instead.

ordinary: The recyclers held through the night. Praise the maintenance.
Mind the copper trim — the Factor's people are about.
A thing that breaks is a kind of heresy. We keep it whole.
stirring: The terminal posts changed. We don't discuss it on shift.
The lower-rind teams stopped filing months ago. The Factor hasn't asked. Neither would I.
Keep your credentials to yourself. They've been lighting the board.
opened: You came up the rind side, didn't you. That gate isn't supposed to open.
Whatever's down there — bring it to the Hall before the Drift, if you're wise.
The air tastes different since the shaft opened. Don't tell me it doesn't.
curve: You've felt it too. The farmland's overhead because the floor curves up to meet it. We don't say it.
Maintenance was never for a city. It's for a hull. Some of us have known a long time.
Don't make me say the word 'ship' on shift. But you know now, don't you.

ordinary: Nothing here is yours for long. Everything moves, eventually.
The Braid's quiet today. Quiet's worth more than noise, sometimes.
Knot at the wrist means I've been everywhere twice. Want a route?
stirring: The Braid's selling the same snag from a dozen directions. The posts changed.
Someone's buying silence about the lower rind. I'm not asking who.
Olo stayed too long in this Nave. We don't do that. Ask her why.
opened: Word travels the Braid faster than you walked it. The shaft's open.
Passage rights into the rind just got dear. Funny, that.
You're carrying something that isn't yours and isn't anyone's. Careful with it.
curve: The Braid always knew the Nave moves. Why d'you think we never settle? Can't own a deck on a ship.
Everything moves because the whole hull's falling between two lights. Now you see it.
You've got the long view now — worth more than any route I could sell you.

ordinary: Mind which doors are warm. The hull tells you, if you listen.
Saturn left things in the deep. We keep them. We don't always understand them.
Years it takes, to read the chambers. You haven't got years.
stirring: That mark you carry — I've scraped it off three walls and it came back.
The deep tunnels are louder than they were. Don't go alone.
Sevin's at the margin. If anyone takes you down, it's her.
opened: So the shaft's yours now. The walls down there are older than the Nave.
The oldest layer of the mark runs all the way down. Follow it, if you dare.
The temperature's wrong in the lower rind. That's not a malfunction.
curve: The rind's the outside of the hull. Always has been. You're standing on the inside of a wheel that turns.
The walls run all the way round and meet themselves. That's how I knew, years back.
Now you've seen the curve, the deep tunnels will make a different kind of sense.

ordinary: The sky-arc's at full today. Good light.
Just passing through. Aren't we all.
stirring: Something's off lately. Can't say what.
People are quieter than they were.
opened: The whole Nave's talking about the rind.
Things are changing. You can feel it.
curve: Can't look at the sky-arc the same way now, can you.
Inside something that moves. Who'd have thought.
